desc_long: The Shine Riders are Coffin Canyon's most opportunistic faction. They win not by destroying their enemies but by taking everything worth having and leaving before the bill comes due. Every unit is built around speed, theft, extraction, or denial — often all four at once.

preferred_states.reason: No authority, no checkpoints, no consequences. Shine Riders are the ecosystem here.
Stripped land full of abandoned claims, loose silver, and desperate sellers. Exactly their market.
Rich towns with distracted owners are prime targets. The more wealth concentrates, the more there is to steal.

neutral_states.reason: Monsters are a hazard, but Rattle Cart redirects them. Gas Masks help. There's still silver in the wild.
Unpleasant but navigable. Gas Masks are standard kit and Suppression works just as well in a dust storm.

opposed_states.reason: Liberty Corps means checkpoints, arrests, and escort denial. Everything the Shine Riders cannot afford.
Monster Rangers patrol actively and don't let extractions slide. Bad for business.
Undead don't carry silver, cursed buildings don't turn, and Claim Markers seem to make things worse.

ideal_scenarios: Thyr extraction races against the clock
Building capture and Silver mine conversion
Asymmetric scenarios where leaving early scores points
challenging_scenarios: High-density monster boards where Suppression is not enough
Scenarios that punish unit loss heavily

faction_features.name: Extraction Specialists
faction_features.effect: Shine Riders units gain +1 die when performing Interact actions to extract Thyr or turn buildings into Silver mines.

faction_features.name: Quick Exit
faction_features.effect: Shine Riders units within 6" of a table edge gain +1 to Disengage rolls. Retreating is never shameful — it is the plan.

faction_features.name: Silver Rush
faction_features.effect: Earn bonus Silver for each building successfully turned and held until end of game.

faction_features.name: Claim Salting
faction_features.effect: Bootboomers may place Claim Markers on Thyr Crystals or Buildings instead of extracting them. Claim Markers trigger on first enemy contact: 1 automatic hit + Suppressed.

faction_features.name: Doomshine
faction_features.effect: Doomshine Casks may appear as objective markers on the board. Shine Riders units carry them without penalty. All other factions treat carried casks as Encumbering (-1" Move). When a Shine Riders unit is destroyed, roll 1d6 — on a 4+, they drop a Doomshine Cask marker at their death location. Any unit may pick it up. Thrallo within 12" of an unsecured cask must pass a Morale test each activation or move toward the nearest cask instead of their intended action.

victory_objectives.description: Score VP for successfully extracting Thyr crystals from the battlefield and exiting via a table edge.
Score VP for converting buildings into Silver mines and holding them until end of game.
Score VP if your Shine Boss exits the table via a table edge while carrying extracted resources.
Score VP for contesting objectives and escaping before heavy resistance arrives.

weapons_note: Unit costs include standard-issue weapons. Weapon names are flavour; mechanical effects are defined by weapon_properties and abilities in core rules.
universal_upgrades_note: Shine Riders may purchase Gas Mask and Spare Parts upgrades from core rules where appropriate. Vehicle units (Wagon Gat, Rattle Cart) may also access vehicle repair upgrades.

units.name: Glowhands
units.lore: Expendable Thyr harvesters. They wear thick gloves because the crystals burn bare skin, and they learned that lesson the hard way. Most of them are missing at least one finger.
units.tactics: Your primary extraction engine. Rush them forward behind faster units and spend every action on Interact. They are tools, not fighters — D0 means one bad round ends them. Quick Draw gives them a fighting chance if something gets close. Sixguns is worth considering if the board is contested; Mounted lets them reach objectives a full turn faster.

optional_upgrades.name: Sixguns
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Replace sidearm with a brace of revolvers. Gain +1 die on ranged attacks up to 8".

optional_upgrades.name: Mounted
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain mounted: +4" Move, Disengage after any attack. Cannot enter Tight Interior or Dense Rubble. Lost if dismounted.

optional_upgrades.name: Doomshine Drunk
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to Fear; auto-pass Morale. On any natural 1 during activation, stumble 1" toward nearest enemy.

units.name: Shard Runners
units.lore: Half courier, half gambler. Shard Runners bet their lives on being faster than bullets. Most of the time they are right. The ones who aren't don't come back to argue about it.
units.tactics: The fastest objective grabbers in the faction. Deploy wide, grab the nearest objective, and angle immediately toward the table edge. Boardwalk Blitzer makes urban boards terrifying — a Shard Runner on a boardwalk covers the whole edge in one activation. Never stand still and trade shots; D0 means they die to a stiff breeze.

optional_upgrades.name: Sixguns
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Replace sidearm with a brace of revolvers. Gain +1 die on ranged attacks up to 8".

optional_upgrades.name: Mounted
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain mounted: +4" Move, Disengage after any attack. Cannot enter Tight Interior or Dense Rubble. Lost if dismounted.

optional_upgrades.name: Doomshine Drunk
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to Fear; auto-pass Morale. On any natural 1 during activation, stumble 1" toward nearest enemy.

units.name: Bootboomers
units.lore: They 'mine' claims the old-fashioned way: by hiding charges in them and walking away. The Bootboomer's favourite phrase is 'their problem now.'
units.tactics: Go forward early, salt as many objectives as possible, then abandon them. The traps do the rest. A salted Thyr Crystal or building forces the enemy to either trigger the trap or give up contesting it — both outcomes work in your favour. Demolition can blow cover and open extraction paths for your Glowhands. Mounted lets them reach and salt objectives a round sooner.

special_mechanics.salted_claim: When this unit successfully Interacts with a Thyr Crystal or Building, it may place a Claim Marker on it instead of extracting. The first non-Shine Rider unit to contact the marked feature suffers 1 automatic hit and becomes Suppressed until end of its next activation. Remove the marker after triggering. Limit: 1 Claim Marker per Bootboomer in play at a time.

optional_upgrades.name: Sixguns
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Replace sidearm with a brace of revolvers. Gain +1 die on ranged attacks up to 8".

optional_upgrades.name: Mounted
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain mounted: +4" Move, Disengage after any attack. Cannot enter Tight Interior or Dense Rubble. Lost if dismounted.

optional_upgrades.name: Doomshine Drunk
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to Fear; auto-pass Morale. On any natural 1 during activation, stumble 1" toward nearest enemy.

units.name: Scrapjack Looters
units.lore: Specialists in ripping value off living targets. Hookblades strip weapons. Magnet hooks pull supplies. Crowbars handle everything else. They have never met a thing they couldn't remove from someone who wanted to keep it.
units.tactics: Target high-value enemy units — Leaders, Specialists, anything with expensive upgrades. A successful Steal destroys the upgrade rather than transferring it, which is usually enough. At D0 they cannot absorb a fight, so use approach routes carefully. Canyon Jackals make excellent escorts — they can neutralize whatever is guarding the target while Scrapjacks do the actual theft.

special_mechanics.steal_faction_rule: When a Scrapjack Looter successfully uses the steal ability, the stolen upgrade is destroyed rather than captured. The target unit loses the upgrade permanently for the remainder of the game.

optional_upgrades.name: Sixguns
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Replace sidearm with a brace of revolvers. Gain +1 die on ranged attacks up to 8".

optional_upgrades.name: Mounted
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain mounted: +4" Move, Disengage after any attack. Cannot enter Tight Interior or Dense Rubble. Lost if dismounted.

optional_upgrades.name: Doomshine Drunk
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to Fear; auto-pass Morale. On any natural 1 during activation, stumble 1" toward nearest enemy.

units.name: Ash Slingers
units.lore: Mounted dust-throwers who learned that the canyon's worst weapon is its air. They fill leather pouches with alkali dust, ride in fast, and throw the canyon itself into your eyes.
units.tactics: Deploy via Scout to hit your priority target with Suppression on Turn 1 before they can react. The unit most likely to contest your Glowhands is the right first target — waste their activation and your extractors work unmolested. Cavalry Disengage means you can never be truly pinned. Gas Masks make them immune to their own dust, which matters when Mustard Gas is in play.

optional_upgrades.name: Sixguns
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Replace sidearm with a brace of revolvers. Gain +1 die on ranged attacks up to 8".

optional_upgrades.name: Doomshine Drunk
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to Fear; auto-pass Morale. On any natural 1 during activation, stumble 1" toward nearest enemy.

units.name: Blast Jack
units.lore: Blast Jack carries more dynamite than sense. The Shine Riders love him for it. Everyone else has learned to keep their distance when he starts smiling.
units.tactics: Use Demolition to destroy cover and open walls for extraction routes. Dynamite Blast panics nearby Monsters — a well-placed throw can redirect a monster away from your Glowhands entirely. Keep him behind cover; Volatile Cargo makes his death a danger to both sides, which most enemies will factor into their decisions. Mounted lets him reach demolition targets faster and escape before things get bad.

special_mechanics.scare_tactics: When Blast Jack successfully hits with dynamite, all Monster units within 6" of the blast must test Morale at -1 die. Failed tests cause those Monsters to move away from the blast center.
special_mechanics.volatile_cargo: If Blast Jack is destroyed, roll 1d6. On 5-6, all units within 3" take 1 automatic damage from exploding dynamite. Affects all factions including Shine Riders.

optional_upgrades.name: Sixguns
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Replace sidearm with a brace of revolvers. Gain +1 die on ranged attacks up to 8".

optional_upgrades.name: Mounted
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain mounted: +4" Move, Disengage after any attack. Cannot enter Tight Interior or Dense Rubble. Lost if dismounted.

optional_upgrades.name: Doomshine Drunk
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to Fear; auto-pass Morale. On any natural 1 during activation, stumble 1" toward nearest enemy.

units.name: Wagon Gat Gunner
units.lore: A gatling gun welded to a wagon frame and pointed at whatever is approaching the extraction team. It does not move quickly. It does not need to.
units.tactics: Deploy in the second rank covering your extractors' flank. Anchor early and Suppress anything that threatens your Glowhands. Immobile means placement is everything — think carefully before you commit. A well-placed Wagon Gat buys multiple turns of free Interact actions by making the contested lane too costly to enter. Spare Parts keeps it functional if the enemy manages to damage it.

optional_upgrades.name: Sixguns
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Replace sidearm with a brace of revolvers. Gain +1 die on ranged attacks up to 8".

optional_upgrades.name: Spare Parts
optional_upgrades.desc_short: May be repaired by adjacent friendly Specialist. Restore 1 Quality on a successful Repair roll.

units.name: Crystal Whisperer
units.lore: Nobody hired her for the Thyr magic. She came with the canyon. She seems to find the caches before anyone else does, which makes her useful and unsettling in equal measure.
units.tactics: Hex the enemy unit most likely to contest your extraction — -1 Quality on a Leader or high-Q fighter can flip a fight entirely. Stealth keeps her alive at range. Unleashed Power and the Wild Magic Table are last resorts; the results are chaotic but occasionally game-changing. Limit 1 per force. Consuming a nearby Thyr Crystal to reroll a bad Wild Magic result can save a round.

special_mechanics.wild_magic_table: Roll 2d6 when using unleashed_power. Results range from Catastrophic Backfire (2) to Thyr Overcharge (12). See Wild Magic Table in core rules.
special_mechanics.thyr_channeling: May consume an adjacent extracted Thyr Crystal to reroll any single Wild Magic result once per game.

optional_upgrades.name: Sixguns
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Replace sidearm with a brace of revolvers. Gain +1 die on ranged attacks up to 8".

optional_upgrades.name: Mounted
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain mounted: +4" Move, Disengage after any attack. Cannot enter Tight Interior or Dense Rubble. Lost if dismounted.

optional_upgrades.name: Doomshine Drunk
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to Fear; auto-pass Morale. On any natural 1 during activation, stumble 1" toward nearest enemy.

units.name: Canyon Jackals
units.lore: The ones you do not see coming. Canyon Jackals run light, fight dirty, and live by one rule: never be the second one to draw. They pick isolated targets, close fast, and are usually gone before their victim hits the ground.
units.tactics: Deploy wide and hunt isolated units — a Leader out of support range, an extractor left alone for one round. Quick Draw means you always strike first when charged, and Backstab rewards attacking targets that haven't activated yet. Pair with Ash Slingers: Suppressed targets grant +2 dice to Backstab instead of +1. Not a brawler — keep them away from grouped enemies and use Stealth to control when the fight starts.

optional_upgrades.name: Sixguns
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Replace sidearm with a brace of revolvers. Gain +1 die on ranged attacks up to 8".

optional_upgrades.name: Mounted
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain mounted: +4" Move, Disengage after any attack. Cannot enter Tight Interior or Dense Rubble. Lost if dismounted.

optional_upgrades.name: Doomshine Drunk
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to Fear; auto-pass Morale. On any natural 1 during activation, stumble 1" toward nearest enemy.

units.name: Sixgun Bruja
units.lore: Nobody knows if she was a witch who found the guns or a gunfighter who found the witchcraft. What the canyon knows is this: her bullets do not stop hurting when they miss. The smoke they leave behind smells like regret and burnt hair, and whatever she shoots at starts falling apart — slowly, methodically, like something important has been quietly removed.
units.tactics: She is not here to kill. She is here to make killing easier for everyone else. Ride her into medium range and start stacking Hexes on the highest-Quality enemy — a Leader, Hero, or Mystic. Lingering Hex means they cannot shake it until she lets them. Pair with Canyon Jackals (who gain bonus dice against debuffed targets) and Ash Slingers (who stack Suppression on top). D1 means she cannot sustain a fight — keep her moving. At +3 VP on death, do not be reckless.

special_mechanics.hexed_sixguns: The Hexed Sixguns count as both a ranged weapon (12") and a Mystic casting tool. Attacks may target abilities directly: declare a named ability on the target — on a successful hit, that ability is silenced until the target's next activation ends.
special_mechanics.smoke_and_spite: Enemy units targeting the Sixgun Bruja with a ranged attack suffer -1 die if they already carry a Hex Marker.
special_mechanics.high_value_target: If the Sixgun Bruja is destroyed, the enemy gains +3 VP.

optional_upgrades.name: Spite Round
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per game: target immediately gains 3 Hex Markers regardless of hit result. The Bruja suffers 1 Quality damage after firing.

optional_upgrades.name: Doomshine Drunk
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to Fear; auto-pass Morale. On any natural 1 during activation, stumble 1" toward nearest enemy.

units.name: Shine Boss
units.lore: Every Shine Riders crew runs on one person's nerve and everyone else's fear of disappointing them. The Shine Boss is that person. They do not stay to fight. They stay until every unit with something valuable has made it to the edge. Then they leave.
units.tactics: Keep the Shine Boss central to your extraction cluster and within 6" of as many units as possible. The aura rewards retreating — use it deliberately. Inspire adds dice when you need a critical Interact or attack to land. Authority keeps panicking units functional under fire. Mounted is the single best upgrade on this unit — it keeps the Boss alive and mobile all game.

special_mechanics.shine_boss_aura: Friendly Shine Riders units within 6" receive: no VP penalty for abandoning objectives, re-roll failed Disengage attempts once per activation, and +2" Movement when moving toward a table edge.

optional_upgrades.name: Spare Horse
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per game, when this unit would be destroyed, roll 1d6. On 4+, it survives with Q1 instead.

optional_upgrades.name: Mounted
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain mounted: +4" Move, Disengage after any attack. Cannot enter Tight Interior or Dense Rubble. Lost if dismounted.

optional_upgrades.name: Doomshine Drunk
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to Fear; auto-pass Morale. On any natural 1 during activation, stumble 1" toward nearest enemy.

units.name: Rattle Cart
units.lore: A roaming Trojan wagon — windmill-driven, loud as sin, dressed up like a monster to bait the canyon's horrors. Nobody inside. Nothing to lose. The Shine Riders built it to be feared, chased, and survived by anyone who isn't standing next to it when something wrong happens.
units.tactics: Two modes. Monster Bait: leave it unanchored, let Windmill Drive pull it across the board, and watch monsters follow it away from your extractors. Sniper Tower: Anchor early, gain +6" Range, and count as Cover for adjacent units. Never use it as a front-line fighter — Titan durability is for absorbing fire while your real plan happens elsewhere. Windmill Drive is mandatory when unanchored; plan deployment accordingly.

special_mechanics.windmill_drive: At the start of each Shine Rider turn, if the Rattle Cart is not Anchored, it must move 3" in a straight line. The controlling player chooses direction. If this movement would take it off the table, it exits (counts as destroyed for VP but does not explode).
special_mechanics.trojan_tower: As an action, the Rattle Cart may Anchor. While Anchored: gains +6" Range (total 24"), counts as in Cover, cannot move until it spends an action to un-Anchor.
special_mechanics.monster_bait: Monsters within 12" suffer -1 die on Morale tests to target units other than the Rattle Cart. If a Monster fails a Morale test within 12", it must move toward the Rattle Cart if able (minimum 3").
special_mechanics.not_a_bomb: The Rattle Cart does not explode when destroyed. It grants normal VP when destroyed.
